name: La Source
notes: Tight hairpin immediately after the start/finish straight. First corner of the lap, major braking zone.

name: Eau Rouge
notes: Famous high-speed left-hander at the bottom of the valley. Taken flat in modern F1 cars.

name: Raidillon
notes: Steep uphill right-hander immediately exiting Eau Rouge. Often grouped with Eau Rouge but a distinct corner.

name: Les Combes
notes: Right-left chicane at the top of the Kemmel hill. Main overtaking spot on the circuit.

name: Rivage
notes: Slow hairpin right-hander, also called Bruxelles. Significant elevation change on exit.

name: Pouhon
notes: Very fast double-apex left-hander. One of the most demanding corners on the calendar at high speed.

name: Fagnes
notes: Right-hand corner in the Fagnes section, medium speed.

name: Campus
notes: Previously called Stavelot exit. Medium-speed right-hander.

name: Stavelot
notes: Right-hand corner leading onto the back section.

name: Paul Frère
notes: Fast left-right combination named after Belgian racing driver and journalist Paul Frère.

name: Blanchimont
notes: Very fast left-hander taken flat or near-flat. Leads onto the final straight.

name: Bus Stop Chicane
notes: Final chicane before the start/finish straight. Left-right-left sequence. Heavy braking from high speed.
